message:
fcp-store: treat k=0 OfflineAccess as fully reconstructable

Before this change OfflineAccess::can_access() returned true for k=0
(consistent with "zero symbols needed to reconstruct zero data"), but
coverage_bps() returned 0 and coverage() returned 0.0.  That left three
public accessors disagreeing about the same object: "I can access this,
but my coverage is 0%."

The guard in coverage_bps/coverage existed only to avoid dividing by
zero, not to express an edge-case policy.  Since a zero-symbol object
is trivially complete, both now return their "fully covered" values:
coverage_bps() -> 10_000 and coverage() -> 1.0.

Existing k=0 tests were asserting the old (inconsistent) behavior and
are updated accordingly.  symbols_needed() already returns 0 for this
case, so no behavioral change there.
